description_long: This church features a rectangular nave with a steeply pitched red-tiled roof and stone masonry walls. At one end, a prominent square bell tower rises above the main structure, adorned with crenellations and narrow arched windows. The facade includes large arched windows and a main entrance, while the side walls are punctuated by smaller windows. The building sits on a grassy plot, suggesting a traditional parish church often found in rural or suburban settings. The stonework and architectural details evoke a historic, possibly Gothic or English parish style. The overall appearance is solid, with a sense of permanence and community gathering.
description: A stone church with a red-tiled roof, square bell tower, and arched windows, set on a grassy base. The structure is traditional and robust, with historic architectural details.
description_view_0: The church is seen from an angle showing the nave, red-tiled roof, and the full height of the bell tower with its crenellations.
description_view_1: This view highlights the bell tower's facade, including its arched windows and the main entrance at the base.
description_view_2: The rear of the church is visible, showing the back wall and the length of the nave with the roof's slope.
description_view_3: The side of the church is shown, emphasizing the length of the nave and the stonework, with the bell tower at the far end.
